name: Burning Man
traditions: Building and burning a massive wooden effigy; art installations; mutant vehicles; theme camps; gift economy; radical self-expression.
historicalBackground: Burning Man began on Baker Beach, San Francisco in 1986 when Larry Harvey and Jerry James burned a wooden figure. It moved to Nevada's Black Rock Desert in 1990.
description: A week-long experiment in community, art, and self-expression in Nevada's Black Rock Desert, culminating in the burning of a massive wooden effigy.
